hero.title: AI code review for startup engineering teams
hero.description: CodeReview AI turns repository snapshots into prioritized findings, risk scores, human-confirmed decisions, and exportable reports before risky code reaches production.
hero.tip: Uploaded code is inspected as text only. Project files are not executed and dependencies are not installed.

stats.title: Built around a practical review budget
stats.description: The current product focuses on bounded repository snapshots, transparent review cost, and repeatable engineering decisions.

items.description: review modes for standard, deep, and security-focused checks

items.title: 25 MB
items.description: maximum ZIP snapshot size for controlled review scope
files indexed per repository snapshot before filtering

introduce.title: One review pipeline, two complementary signals
introduce.label: How it works
introduce.description: Fast, explainable rules catch known patterns first. The LLM then reasons across modules, data boundaries, and repository context.

items.title: Deterministic rule scan
items.description: Surfaces credential patterns, risky execution, suppressed diagnostics, unresolved markers, and substantial duplicated blocks with rule evidence.

items.title: Repository-aware LLM review
items.description: Profiles the stack, reviews bounded file groups, and performs a dedicated cross-file pass over APIs, auth, persistence, permissions, and payments.

items.title: Human-confirmed report
items.description: Triage findings as open, needs review, ignored, or fixed. Risk scores and exports always reflect the latest confirmation state.

workflow.title: A visible review loop from upload to export
workflow.description: Every stage has a clear purpose and produces evidence for the next decision.

items.title: 1. Submit code
items.description: Upload a ZIP snapshot and choose standard, deep, or security-focused review.

items.title: 2. Secure indexing
items.description: Reject unsafe paths and archive bombs, redact secret-like values, and record reviewed versus ignored files.

items.title: 3. Rule scan
items.description: Run explainable checks for standards, repeated logic, security signals, and potential runtime risks.

items.title: 4. LLM review
items.description: Build a repository profile, review file groups, and reason across critical modules.

items.title: 5. Classify issues
items.description: Normalize severity, category, confidence, location, evidence, and recommended remediation.

items.title: 6. Human confirmation
items.description: Engineers validate uncertain findings and mark each issue open, ignored, or fixed.

items.title: 7. Export results
items.description: Persist triage decisions and export live Markdown or JSON with the current risk state.

benefits.title: Designed for signal, safety, and engineering decisions
benefits.label: Review quality
benefits.description: The product favors traceable evidence and controlled scope over speculative volume.

items.title: Low-noise by design
items.description: Ignored files are visible, duplicate findings are collapsed, and low-confidence items are routed to human review.

items.title: Cross-file reasoning
items.description: Critical API, auth, permission, payment, schema, and model files are reviewed together instead of as isolated snippets.

items.title: No uploaded-code execution
items.description: The service does not run project code, install project packages, or expose uploaded source through public assets.

features.title: Coverage that matches real review work
features.description: Findings are organized around the concerns senior engineers actually triage.

items.title: Correctness
items.description: Bugs, invalid assumptions, missing validation, and error-handling gaps.

items.title: Security and permissions
items.description: Credential exposure, injection surfaces, auth boundaries, and access-control risks.

items.title: Standards
items.description: Suppressed diagnostics, unresolved implementation markers, and maintainability conventions.

items.title: Duplicate logic
items.description: Substantial repeated code blocks that should converge behind one tested implementation.

items.title: Potential risk
items.description: Dynamic execution, swallowed failures, unsafe data flow, and operational blind spots.

items.title: Architecture and tests
items.description: Cross-module coupling, persistence boundaries, performance concerns, and missing test coverage.

faq.title: Code review product questions
faq.description: Clear boundaries make the report easier to trust.

items.question: How are credits used?
items.answer: Standard reviews use 10 credits, security-focused reviews use 20 credits, and deep reviews use 25 credits. The workspace checks your balance before starting and consumes credits only after a successful review.

items.question: Does the service execute uploaded code?
items.answer: No. CodeReview AI reads a bounded ZIP snapshot, filters files, redacts common secret patterns, and sends selected source text to the configured review model.

items.question: What does the rule scan cover?
items.answer: It currently covers credential-like values, risky dynamic execution, raw HTML rendering, empty catch blocks, static-analysis suppressions, unresolved markers, and substantial cross-file duplication.

items.question: Why is human confirmation part of the workflow?
items.answer: Static rules and LLM reasoning can both need repository-specific context. Human status changes are persisted and immediately reflected in the risk score and exported report.

items.question: Can it review a GitHub pull request?
items.answer: The current product reviews ZIP repository snapshots. GitHub App and pull-request write-back are reserved for a later integration phase.

cta.title: Start with one repository snapshot
cta.description: Get a structured review, confirm the findings, and export a report your team can act on.
